Photo by Mohammad Rahmani on Unsplash“What’s the best folder structure for a Flutter project?”
This is one of the most frequently asked questions in the Flutter community.
Search on GitHub, browse open-source projects, or watch YouTube tutorials, and you’ll find dozens of different answers.
Some developers organize everything like this:
lib/
├── screens/
├── models/
├── services/
├── widgets/
├── providers/
└── utils/Others prefer something completely different:
lib/
├── features/
├── core/
└── shared/So who’s right?
The honest answer is:
Both… and neither.
Because choosing a project structure isn’t about following trends.
It’s about understanding how your application is going to grow.
In this chapter of the Flutter Architecture Masterclass, we’ll compare Layer-Based and Feature-Based project structures, understand their strengths and weaknesses, and discuss which one makes the most sense for modern Flutter applications.
First, Let’s Clear Up Another Myth
